item.title: Witkoff says any Iran nuclear deal should last indefinitely, Axios reports
item.description: Feb 25 (Reuters) - U.S. President Donald Trump's special envoy Steve Witkoff told a private gathering on Tuesday that the administration is demanding any nuclear deal with Iran should remain in effect indefinitely, Axios reported on Wednesday, citing a U.S. official and two others with knowledge of the comments.

item.title: Vance warns Iran to take US threats 'seriously'
item.description:
US Vice President JD Vance told Iran Wednesday to take Washington's threats of military action "seriously," a day after President Donald Trump appeared to build the case for war in his State of the Union address.

As US forces mass in the Middle East, Trump claimed in his speech to Congress on Tuesday that Iran was seeking to develop missiles that can strike the United States.

item.title: Modi says India stands 'firmly' with Israel during visit
item.description:
Prime Minister Narendra Modi told lawmakers in Jerusalem on Wednesday that India stands "firmly" with Israel following Hamas's attack on October 7, 2023.

Modi also met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu during a two-day visit aimed at deepening trade and defence ties that has drawn criticism at home.

"I... carry with me the deepest condolences of the people of India for every life lost and for every family whose world was shattered in the barbaric terrorist attack by Hamas on October 7," Modi said in the Knesset, the Israeli parliament.

item.title: Berlinale meet called over film director's anti-Israel speech
item.description:
German culture officials plan to summon organisers of the Berlin film festival Thursday after the event was rocked this year by controversy over the Israel-Palestinian conflict and Gaza war.

Bild daily reported that Tricia Tuttle, the US director of the Berlinale, is expected to be sacked at the meeting, citing sources close to the KBB, the state-owned company that runs the festival.

The KBB dismissed the report, telling AFP in a short statement: "We believe this is fake news."

item.title: Vance tells Fox News Trump still prefers diplomatic solution with Iran
item.description:
Feb 25 (Reuters) - U.S. Vice President JD Vance said on Wednesday that President Donald Trump still preferred a diplomatic solution with Iran and that he hoped Iranians took that seriously in their negotiations on Thursday.

"The President has been as crystal clear as he could be, Iran can't have a nuclear weapon... He's going to try to accomplish it diplomatically," Vance told Fox News in an interview.

(Reporting by Bhargav Acharya in Toronto; Editing by Caitlin Webber)

item.title: Australia urges diplomats' dependents to leave Lebanon, Israel: What to know
item.description: Australia's order comes just a day after the US directed non-essential personnel at its embassy in Lebanon to depart.

item.title: Australia urges diplomats' dependents to leave Lebanon, Israel: What to know
item.description: Australia's order comes just a day after the US directed non-essential personnel at its embassy in Lebanon to depart.

item.title: Australia urges diplomats' dependents to leave Lebanon, Israel: What to know
item.description: Australia's order comes just a day after the US directed non-essential personnel at its embassy in Lebanon to depart.

item.title: Australia urges diplomats' dependents to leave Lebanon, Israel: What to know
item.description: Australia's order comes just a day after the US directed non-essential personnel at its embassy in Lebanon to depart.

item.title: Oil, markets digest escalation risks ahead of US-Iran talks
item.description: With critical US-Iran talks scheduled for Thursday, markets continue adjusting to the risks posed by an escalation in the Middle East should diplomacy falter.

item.title: Oil, markets digest escalation risks ahead of US-Iran talks
item.description: With critical US-Iran talks scheduled for Thursday, markets continue adjusting to the risks posed by an escalation in the Middle East should diplomacy falter.

item.title: Oil, markets digest escalation risks ahead of US-Iran talks
item.description: With critical US-Iran talks scheduled for Thursday, markets continue adjusting to the risks posed by an escalation in the Middle East should diplomacy falter.

item.title: Oil, markets digest escalation risks ahead of US-Iran talks
item.description: With critical US-Iran talks scheduled for Thursday, markets continue adjusting to the risks posed by an escalation in the Middle East should diplomacy falter.

item.title: Israeli settlers burn tents, vehicles in West Bank village, say residents
item.description:
SUSIYA, West Bank Feb 25 (Reuters) - Israeli settlers set fire to vehicles and tents in the Palestinian village of Susiya on Tuesday night, residents said, in the latest incidentof settler violence against Palestinians in the Israeli-occupied West Bank.

Videos verified by Reuters showed a masked group of men, who residents said were Israeli settlers, approaching the village near the city of Hebron, and later burning vehicles and Palestinian property.

item.title: US Treasury issues fresh Iran-related sanctions, website shows
item.description:
Feb 25 (Reuters) - The U.S. Treasury Department on Wednesday imposed new Iran-related sanctions on four individuals, several entities and tankers, according to a notice on its website.

(Reporting by Bhargav Acharya in Toronto; Editing by Daphne Psaledakis)

item.title: US F-22 fighter jets reach Israel as military pressure builds on Iran
item.description: The US Air Force's tier-one stealth fighter last June penetrated Iranian airspace undetected to pave the way for B-2 bomber strikes on key nuclear facilities, Pentagon officials said at the time.

item.title: US F-22 fighter jets reach Israel as military pressure builds on Iran
item.description: The US Air Force's tier-one stealth fighter last June penetrated Iranian airspace undetected to pave the way for B-2 bomber strikes on key nuclear facilities, Pentagon officials said at the time.

item.title: US F-22 fighter jets reach Israel as military pressure builds on Iran
item.description: The US Air Force's tier-one stealth fighter last June penetrated Iranian airspace undetected to pave the way for B-2 bomber strikes on key nuclear facilities, Pentagon officials said at the time.

item.title: US F-22 fighter jets reach Israel as military pressure builds on Iran
item.description: The US Air Force's tier-one stealth fighter last June penetrated Iranian airspace undetected to pave the way for B-2 bomber strikes on key nuclear facilities, Pentagon officials said at the time.

item.title: Iran negotiators head to Geneva for US talks, president strikes hopeful tone
item.description:
An Iranian delegation headed by its top diplomat set off for Geneva on Wednesday for talks with the US, as the Islamic republic's president struck an upbeat tone about the prospect for a negotiated agreement to avert fresh conflict.

Donald Trump has repeatedly threatened strikes if Iran fails to cut a deal on its atomic programme, and in his State of the Union address on Tuesday, the US president accused Tehran of "sinister nuclear ambitions" after he ordered a massive military deployment around the Gulf.
